# ARC-QUIC-CRT-0022 - QUIC CRT server client-auth transcript/runtime prerequisite architecture

## Purpose

Describe the smallest honest public server-auth gate on the existing SslServerAuthenticationOptions carrier, reusing the internal managed transcript/runtime prerequisite to emit TLS 1.3 CertificateRequest and accept client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished through the existing bridge without widening broader client-auth behavior.

## Scope

This architecture slice now covers the public server-auth gate plus the internal transcript/runtime prerequisite: CertificateRequest emission, client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished acceptance, and callback-driven acceptance on the existing bridge seam. Broader chain/revocation customization and broader PKI behavior remain out of scope.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0124

## Design Summary

The narrowest honest step is to open the existing public server-auth carrier only where the already-landed internal TLS transcript and bridge machinery can prove the behavior. The transcript owner now has a role-aware branch for an inbound client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished sequence after ClientHello when server client-auth is enabled, the key schedule emits one local CertificateRequest, and the transport bridge owns the existing callback seam that can accept or reject the presented client certificate without introducing a broader PKI policy surface. Broader chain/revocation customization, broader PKI behavior, and any public API widening beyond the existing carrier remain deferred.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicServerConnectionOptionsValidator.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicListenerHost.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0124.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/QUIC/REQ-QUIC-API-0005.cs

## Risks

- If the validator allows ClientCertificateRequired without the callback seam, the public gate would overclaim a client-auth floor the runtime cannot exercise.
- If the client-certificate callback is modeled as a trust-store replacement, the slice will overclaim PKI behavior that was explicitly deferred.
- If the public gate is left closed after the internal floor exists, the supported carrier will remain artificially blocked.
